originalAlarm.description: Assigned value is garbage or undefined

presenceCondition:
(or (and (not CONFIG_FEATURE_LS_RECURSIVE) (not CONFIG_FEATURE_LS_SORTFILES))
    (and (not CONFIG_FEATURE_LS_RECURSIVE) CONFIG_FEATURE_LS_SORTFILES)
    (and CONFIG_FEATURE_LS_RECURSIVE (not CONFIG_FEATURE_LS_SORTFILES))
    (and CONFIG_FEATURE_LS_RECURSIVE CONFIG_FEATURE_LS_SORTFILES))
model:
(define-fun CONFIG_FEATURE_LS_RECURSIVE () Bool
  true)
(define-fun CONFIG_FEATURE_LS_SORTFILES () Bool
  false)
reasoning: 2631486f1bf.c L67: curr assigned garbage or undefined value from malloc (null from malloc). The bug only manifests when CONFIG_FEATURE_LS_SORTFILES=T (curr is used in the sort-files code path). PC covers all four combinations of RECURSIVE and SORTFILES — these cancel out to true, including CONFIG_FEATURE_LS_SORTFILES=F where L67 may not exist. PC is over-constrained (too broad — includes SORTFILES=F configs where the code at L67 is absent). New bug.
